episode_title: #1 on Amazon… and Almost No One Owns the Stock
speaker: Chris Camillo
speaker_attribution_reason: Chris originated the trade reveal; multiple first-person 'I own / I bought / I'm long' statements throughout the segment.
catalyst_or_condition: Neato squishy trend continuing through May earnings; Schilling ramping production
bull_thesis: Neato is #1 on Amazon multiple positions; high-margin, easy-to-manufacture, mass distribution already in place; Gladstone explicitly named Schilling as a needle-mover in early-Feb earnings; analog of New Brands/Slime/Elmer's trade
bear_thesis_or_risks: Holding-co opacity (24-25 portfolio companies); supply chain ramp from China; knockoffs flooding market; Tim Hortons-style hidden-drag risk
key_quotes: [1:34] I own the company. I'm not planning to sell the company anytime soon uh until I see this trade through.
[12:50] full transparency, you know, I I'm long the stock. I I won't be selling into any of this in the next few days.
[15:51] I'm kind of excited about this trade guys. It's as old school social arb as it gets.
[20:49] I I I I bought a pretty decent amount of the of the of the stock, but I like I said, I you will not see me selling this next week.
[21:00] I'm hoping to hold. This is honestly one I will hold most likely as long as the product continues to trend.
summary: Central social-arb trade reveal. Owns meaningful position; holding through May earnings catalyst. Acknowledges as 'fun trade' due to small float.
contradictions_or_tensions: Dave (host) is openly skeptical that Schilling can ramp manufacturing in time; Chris disagrees.
notes: Chris frames as a 'fun trade' due to small float, suggesting position size is smaller than his AMZN/BE positions despite high excitement.

episode_title: #1 on Amazon… and Almost No One Owns the Stock
speaker: Chris Camillo
speaker_attribution_reason: Repeated 'my Amazon trade', 'my Amazon position', 'my lever position', 'I'm picking one and going into it highly levered'.
catalyst_or_condition: Iran war ending / oil prices stabilizing
bull_thesis: AI super-cycle; Amazon's $30-50B OpenAI investment ensures Trainium chip + retail integration; Anthropic relationship; AMZN at top of AI trade with super-leverable liquid market.
bear_thesis_or_risks: Iran war + oil price spike crushing transport/logistics; potential AI-driven job losses crushing consumer; Wall Street pricing oil headwinds.
key_quotes: [32:55] The second that we see any light at the end of this tunnel in this Iran war, the second it looks like this thing is nearing an end, I think I'm going even deeper into Amazon, guys.
[33:42] I love Amazon today more than I liked it when we did our episode a few weeks ago.
[34:18] I would pour into it in the 220s.
[40:02] I haven't sold any of the my Amazon position. I haven't sold any of the lever position I have in into Amazon.
[40:23] I feel like I want to own more, not less of Amazon.
summary: Owns leveraged Amazon position; conviction increased over prior weeks; will pour in at $220s; will lever further when war ends.
